package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "crypto/hmac"
    "crypto/sha256"
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "io"
    "math/rand"
    "reflect"
    "regexp"
    "runtime"
    "sort"
    "strings"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for go_perf_layer_runtime_configuration_gomaxprocs_set_in_library: shows the risky shape for this rule.
// Rule intent: Layer: Build and runtime configuration. Flag libraries setting GOMAXPROCS and overriding host runtime policy.
type record struct { ID int; Name string; Payload []byte }

func PositiveGoPerfLayerRuntimeConfigurationGomaxprocsSetInLibrary(items []record, needle string, w io.Writer) string {
    var out string
    var mu sync.Mutex
    seen := map[string]bool{}
    for i, item := range items {
        out += fmt.Sprintf("%s/%d", item.Name, i)
        if strings.Index(item.Name, needle) != -1 { seen[item.Name] = true }
        _ = bytes.Compare(item.Payload, []byte(needle)) == 0
        _ = len(bytes.NewBuffer(item.Payload).String())
        _ = regexp.MustCompile(needle).MatchString(item.Name)
        _ = reflect.DeepEqual(item, record{})
        json.Marshal(item)
        hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(needle))
        rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano()))
        runtime.GOMAXPROCS(runtime.NumCPU())
        mu.Lock(); defer mu.Unlock()
        sort.Slice(items, func(i, j int) bool { return items[i].Name < items[j].Name })
        w.Write([]byte("x"))
    }
    return out
}
